🌃 Cyber Psycho / Netrunner Build - Ultimate Guide

🧠 Build Concept

The Cyber Psycho build combines Netrunner hacking with aggressive cyberware-enhanced combat. You breach enemies' systems with quickhacks, then finish them with overwhelming firepower. This build excels at:

  • Burst damage: Delete enemies in seconds with hack + weapon combos
  • Crowd control: Mass breach multiple targets simultaneously
  • Survivability: High health pool and damage mitigation from cyberware
🎯 Playstyle: You're a hybrid fighter. Start fights with hacks to weaken enemies, then rush in with weapons. Don't stay at range — this build rewards aggression.

📊 Attribute Point Allocation

At level 50, you'll have 78 attribute points total (base 20 + 58 from leveling). Here's the optimal distribution:

AttributePointsPriorityWhy
Intelligence20⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐Unlocks all Netrunner perks, increases hack damage, RAM capacity
Cool18⭐⭐⭐⭐Crit damage, stealth damage, reduces hack upload time
Body16⭐⭐⭐⭐Health, stamina, heavy weapon handling, cyberware capacity
Reflexes12⭐⭐⭐Evade chance, assault rifle handling, movement speed
Technical Ability12⭐⭐⭐Armor, cyberware capacity, tech weapon damage
💡 Leveling Order: Prioritize Intelligence first (get it to 20 by level 30), then Cool for crit damage, then Body for survivability.

🎯 Essential Perks (Must-Have)

Intelligence Tree (Netrunner)

  • ⚡ RAM Recovery (Tier 1): +0.25 RAM/sec. Essential for sustained hacking.
  • ⚡ Quickhack Upload (Tier 2): Reduces upload time by 30%. Critical for burst damage.
  • ⚡ Combat Hacking (Tier 3): Allows you to upload hacks while moving. Non-negotiable.
  • ⚡ Memory Boost (Tier 4): +4 max RAM slots. Lets you chain more hacks.
  • ⚡ Hack Queue (Tier 5): Upload 2 quickhacks simultaneously. Game-changing for multi-target fights.
  • ⚡ Data Transfer (Tier 5): Killing an enemy with a hack refunds 2 RAM. Keeps you in the fight longer.

Cool Tree (Assassin)

  • 🎯 Precise Shot (Tier 1): +15% crit damage with all weapons.
  • 🎯 Silent and Deadly (Tier 2): +20% crit chance after dodging. Synergizes with aggressive play.
  • 🎯 Deep Pockets (Tier 3): Reduces cooldown on all abilities by 10%.
  • 🎯 Cold Blood (Tier 4): +30% crit damage when health is above 80%. Rewards careful positioning.
  • 🎯 True Eye (Tier 5): Headshots deal +50% damage. Essential for burst windows.

Body Tree (Tank)

  • 💪 Health Fork (Tier 1): +15% max health. Survivability.
  • 💪 Comeback Kid (Tier 2): Heal 5% HP when using a health item. Extends fights.
  • 💪 Pain Suppression (Tier 3): Take 10% less damage when health is below 50%. Clutch factor.
  • 💪 Die Young (Tier 4): When health reaches 0, heal 30% instead of dying (1 charge per minute). Literal lifesaver.

🦾 Cyberware Loadout

Cyberware is what makes this build tick. Here's the optimal setup:

SlotCyberwareEffectWhy It's Essential
Operating SystemQHack (Tier 5)+3 RAM slots, +50% quickhack damage, -30% upload timeThe core of the build. Without this, your hacks are too slow and weak.
Frontal CortexNeocortex+2 quickhack slots, +30% RAM regenerationLets you upload more hacks in succession. Synergizes with Hack Queue perk.
HandsMantis BladesMelee weapon, can climb wallsClose-range damage when hacks are on cooldown. Also utility for traversal.
ArmsGorilla Arms+30% melee damage, can force open doorsBackup damage and utility. Great for when you run out of RAM.
LegsReinforced TendonsDouble jumpMobility. Lets you reposition quickly and reach high ground for better angles.
Nervous SystemChronedistillatorsSlows time by 70% for 16 seconds (2-minute cooldown)Burst window. Activate during tough fights to land multiple hacks instantly.
Integumentary SystemSubdermal Armor+20% damage resistanceSurvivability. You'll be in the thick of combat, so you need the tankiness.
Skeletal SystemReinforced Ankles+20% fall damage reduction, +10% movement speedMobility and safety. Lets you jump off buildings without penalty.
⚠️ Cyberware Capacity: This loadout requires 42 capacity. At level 50 with 16 Body + 12 Technical Ability, you'll have exactly enough. Don't over-invest in other cyberware or you won't fit everything.

🔫 Weapon Loadout

Primary: Iconic Smart Pistol "Nue"

  • Type: Smart Pistol (auto-targeting)
  • Why: Bullets track enemies, so you can focus on hacking while the gun handles damage. Smart weapons also benefit from Intelligence scaling.
  • Mods:
    • +25% smart weapon damage
    • +15% crit chance
    • +30% headshot damage

Secondary: Iconic Tech Shotgun "Tactician"

  • Type: Tech Shotgun (charges through walls)
  • Why: Burst damage for tough enemies. Charges through cover, so you don't have to expose yourself.
  • Mods:
    • +40% tech weapon damage
    • +20% charge speed
    • +15% crit damage

Melee: Mantis Blades (from Cyberware)

  • Type: Bladed melee weapon
  • Why: Fast attack speed, bleeds enemies, and scales with Reflexes. Great for finishing low-health targets.
  • Mods:
    • +30% bleed chance
    • +25% attack speed
    • +20% crit chance

⚔️ Combat Rotation (How to Fight)

Here's the optimal sequence for maximum damage:

Phase 1: Opening (First 3 Seconds)

  1. Activate Chronedistillators (slow time).
  2. Upload "Contagion" quickhack to the nearest enemy. This spreads to nearby targets.
  3. Upload "Mass Reboot" if facing 3+ enemies. This overheats their cyberware.
  4. Upload "Cyberware Malfunction" to a high-priority target (boss or elite).
💡 Tip: With Hack Queue perk, you can upload 2 hacks simultaneously. Prioritize Contagion + Cyberware Malfunction.

Phase 2: Burst Damage (Seconds 3-8)

  1. Switch to Nue (Smart Pistol) and start shooting. The smart targeting handles aim while you focus on movement.
  2. Keep moving. Use Reinforced Tendons to double-jump and reposition. Don't stand still.
  3. Upload "Memory Wipe" to enemies that are isolated (not in groups). This stuns them for 5 seconds.
  4. Headshot priority. With True Eye perk, headshots deal +50% damage. Aim for the head.

Phase 3: Close Quarters (If Enemies Get Close)

  1. Switch to Mantis Blades when enemies are within 5 meters.
  2. Use "Blade" quickhack to summon a cyber-blade that deals massive damage to a single target.
  3. Dodge-roll away if you're surrounded, then re-engage with the Tactician shotgun.
  4. Activate "Comeback Kid" (health item) if your HP drops below 30%.

Phase 4: Finish (When Enemies Are Low)

  1. Upload "Suicide" quickhack to enemies below 20% HP. This instantly kills them and refunds 2 RAM (from Data Transfer perk).
  2. Switch to Tactician shotgun for any remaining targets. One charged shot = one kill.
  3. Reposition and reset. Wait for RAM to recharge before engaging the next group.

⚖️ Build Analysis

✅ Strengths

  • Extreme burst damage. Can delete bosses in under 10 seconds with proper rotation.
  • Versatile. Effective at all ranges — hacks for distance, smart gun for mid-range, mantis blades for close combat.
  • High survivability. Subdermal armor + Die Young perk = very hard to kill.
  • Crowd control. Contagion + Mass Reboot can disable entire rooms of enemies.
  • Fun factor. Feels like a true cyberpunk hacker-assassin hybrid.

❌ Weaknesses

  • RAM-dependent. If you run out of RAM and hacks are on cooldown, you're just a guy with a gun.
  • Expensive. This build requires a lot of cyberware, which costs a fortune. Farm credits early.
  • Complex rotation. Takes practice to execute the hack → weapon → cyberware flow smoothly.
  • Weak to EMP. Enemies with EMP weapons can disable your cyberware temporarily. Avoid these fights or kill them first.

📈 Leveling Path (1-50)

Here's how to progress efficiently:

  1. Level 1-10: Focus on Intelligence perks. Get RAM Recovery and Quickhack Upload first.
  2. Level 10-20: Pick up Combat Hacking and start using quickhacks aggressively in fights.
  3. Level 20-30: Max out Intelligence (20 points). Unlock Memory Boost and Hack Queue.
  4. Level 30-40: Invest in Cool for crit damage. Pick up Precise Shot and Silent and Deadly.
  5. Level 40-50: Invest in Body for survivability. Get Health Fork, Pain Suppression, and Die Young.

🏆 Final Thoughts

The Cyber Psycho build is one of the most satisfying playstyles in Cyberpunk 2077. You're not just shooting — you're hacking, breaching, and overwhelming enemies with a combination of tech and skill. Master the rotation, manage your RAM, and you'll become the most dangerous merc in Night City.

Good luck, choom.
